exhibitors.name: 12Port Inc
exhibitors.description: 12Port delivers agentless Privileged Access Management that locks down credentials and brokers access without credential exposure across users, automation and applications. Protect access, secure accounts, monitor and contain threats, enforce zero trust and prove compliance—without added complexity.
exhibitors.roast: Agentless PAM is enterprise theater: a vault, a broker, and a spreadsheet of who touched prod—stuff Terraform and a weekend OAuth proxy can approximate until procurement notices.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a minimal PAM demo: HashiCorp Vault or OpenBao for secrets, short-lived creds via your cloud IAM, and SSH/RDP session recording with audit logs; add a simple policy UI that maps roles to targets.

exhibitors.name: 1Password
exhibitors.description: Trusted by over 175,000 businesses and millions of consumers, 1Password is reimagining identity security for SaaS-forward and AI-powered organizations, delivering a suite of solutions that enable business agility.
exhibitors.roast: A very nice password manager with a marketing team that discovered the word 'AI-powered'—still fundamentally a vault, autofill, and team sharing you could ship with Bitwarden and a designer.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype a team password vault: Bitwarden-compatible server or Vaultwarden, SSO via OIDC, shared collections, and an audit export CSV for SOC 2 evidence—no AI required.

exhibitors.name: A-LIGN
exhibitors.description: A-LIGN is the leading cybersecurity compliance partner, trusted by over 5,700 organizations worldwide to navigate the complexities of compliance, audit, and risk. A-LIGN delivers high-quality, efficient audits across frameworks including SOC 2, ISO 27001, FedRAMP, CMMC, ISO 42001, PCI, and HITRUST.
exhibitors.roast: You are not buying security; you are buying PDFs with letterhead. Auditors are the original compliance-as-a-service—and the product is mostly calendars and stubbornness.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Spin up an internal SOC 2 control matrix in a spreadsheet or Notion: map controls to evidence (tickets, logs, screenshots), assign owners, and automate reminders—mock what GRC tools charge six figures to host.

exhibitors.name: A2SECURE I RISKBLADE
exhibitors.description: A2SECURE is an MSSP, a cybersecurity pure player that acts as a strategic partner for organisations operating in highly regulated sectors. Riskblade is the TPRM solution that guarantees real control over the digital supply chain and compliance.
exhibitors.roast: MSSP plus a TPRM module is 'we email your vendors a Google Form and call it supply chain resilience'—Riskblade is questionnaire cosplay with a Madrid accent.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a vendor-risk tracker: vendor list, questionnaire templates, document upload, scoring rubric, and renewal dates—export to CSV for the board slide that says 'digital supply chain'.

exhibitors.name: AAEON
exhibitors.description: AAEON is a leading developer of hardware and intelligent automated services for premier OEM/ODMs and system integrators worldwide. AAEON also offers customized end-to-end services from initial product conceptualization and product development.
exhibitors.roast: They ship boards, boxes, and custom silicon dreams—your weekend vibe-code can't fab a PCB or pass EMI.

exhibitors.name: AD Consulting SPA
exhibitors.description: AD Consulting is an IT consulting company on digital transformation, delivering tailored cloud, cybersecurity, and advisory solutions. Defenix by AD provides centralized IT/OT visibility, real-time monitoring, threat detection, actionable insights.
exhibitors.roast: Another integrator selling 'Defenix' visibility—usually Grafana, a few agents, and PowerPoint with the word OT in bold.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up OT-ish visibility cheap: syslog + Zeek or Suricata on a span port, ship to OpenSearch, build three dashboards (auth failures, lateral SMB, PLC chatter if present).

exhibitors.name: AEWIN TECH INC.
exhibitors.description: AEWIN is an industry-leading ODM/OEM designer and manufacturer of off-the-shelf (OTS) network appliances and storage servers. AEWIN can provide flexible design and customization options for NGFW, UTM, ADC, edge computing, cloud, edge AI, and storage.
exhibitors.roast: They bend metal and PCIe lanes so your firewall vendor can slap a logo on a 1U toaster—atoms, not prompts.

exhibitors.name: AIBound
exhibitors.description: AIBound is the unified AI security platform that discovers, tests, and protects every model, agent, and AI identity so enterprises can safely scale AI in production.
exhibitors.roast: Unified AI security for models and agents is turtles all the way down—LLM guards guarding LLMs until someone ships grep for embeddings.

exhibitors.name: AIC
exhibitors.description: AIC is a global leader in the design and manufacturing of high-performance server and storage solutions. With nearly 30 years of expertise, AIC is renowned for its high-density storage servers, storage server barebones, and Al storage solutions.
exhibitors.roast: Racks, trays, and thermal nightmares—when your product weighs 40kg, Cursor stops being the threat model.

exhibitors.name: AIONCLOUD
exhibitors.description: AIONCLOUD is MONITORAPP’s comprehensive SECaaS platform. Leveraging a global edge infrastructure, AIONCLOUD delivers subscription-based solutions, from network to endpoint, including WAAP, WSPC, SWG, CASB, RBI, ZTNA, EDR, etc.
exhibitors.roast: WAAP/SWG/CASB at the edge is real plumbing—still a crowded lane next to Cloudflare and Zscaler, but not a vibe-coded CRUD.

exhibitors.name: AIVESP
exhibitors.description: AIVESP is the company behind Olivia, delivering AI-driven consulting solutions to optimize industrial processes, enhance operational efficiency, and enable advanced forecasting capabilities.
exhibitors.roast: AI-driven consulting to optimize industrial processes—PowerPoint with a torch and 'forecasting' in the subtitle.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace slideware with a Jupyter notebook: pull plant telemetry CSVs, forecast with Prophet or ARIMA, and export one executive chart—same story, fraction of the day rate.

exhibitors.name: AQtive Guard by SandboxAQ
exhibitors.description: At the Frontier of Trust, we expose AI and cryptographic risk and enforce real-time guardrails from code to agents. We help enterprises discover, assess, and enable Machine Trust at scale - where exposure ends, trust begins.
exhibitors.roast: Crypto-risk dashboards for agents is GRC with quantum seasoning—still mostly inventory, policy text, and red/yellow tiles.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Make a repo inventory tool: scan for deprecated crypto (TLS versions, weak curves, MD5/SHA1), parse package manifests for known weak deps, emit a risk report grouped by team owner from CODEOWNERS.

exhibitors.name: ARCON TechSolutions
exhibitors.description: ARCON provides enterprise information risk control solutions, specializing in Privileged Access Management and Continuous Risk Assessment to help organizations identify, predict, and prevent technology risks.
exhibitors.roast: PAM is vaults, sessions, and break-glass theater—critical, but it's the same rotating password with better UX.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype a PAM-lite: vault storing secrets in KMS-wrapped blobs, short-lived cred issuance API, session recording metadata (who/when/what host), and periodic access review export.

exhibitors.name: AREXDATA
exhibitors.description: Arexdata is a manufacturer specialising in Data Security Platforms. Arexdata DSPM enables companies to audit, standardise, classify and protect information, providing centralised end-to-end visibility and traceability.
exhibitors.roast: DSPM is regex, metadata, and DLP policies pretending to be strategy—classification is forever a moving target.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship a file classifier MVP: walk object storage, sample MIME/extension, run PII regex detectors + entropy checks, write results to a table with false-positive review flags and a simple quarantine tagger job.

exhibitors.name: AT&T Business
exhibitors.description: Why AT&T Business? See how ultra-fast, reliable fiber and 5G connectivity protected by built-in security give you a new level of confidence in the possibilities of your network. Let our experts work with you to solve your challenges and outcomes.
exhibitors.roast: Fiber and 5G are trenches, permits, and spectrum—your outage is a backhoe, not a prompt injection.

exhibitors.name: AWARE7 GmbH
exhibitors.description: AWARE7 GmbH is an ISO27001 certified cybersecurity company headquartered in Gelsenkirchen, Germany. We focus on awareness, offensive services and information security consulting.
exhibitors.roast: ISO27001-certified phishing slides: necessary, unkillable, and impossible to differentiate on a trade-show floor.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up a measurable phishing program: baseline click rate, segment by department, A/B test two email templates, and publish a single CSV of results + remediation actions—no ‘awareness portal’ vendor required.

exhibitors.name: Absolute Security
exhibitors.description: We Stop Downtime Absolute Security is the only provider of self-healing, intelligent security solutions that empower enterprises, governments, educational institutions, and more to stay resilient in the face of growing and evolving cyber threats.
exhibitors.roast: Annoyingly persistent: firmware-resident agents that survive wipe-and-reimage are the opposite of a CRUD app—still, the clock ticks as OS vendors tighten attestation.

exhibitors.name: AbuseIPDB
exhibitors.description: AbuseIPDB delivers real-time IP threat intelligence and reputation data on malicious infrastructure. With a powerful API, on-premises threat feeds, and hundreds of integrations for SOC/SIEM/SOAR and more, security teams can easily automate enrichments and take proactive actions to block threats.
exhibitors.roast: IP reputation as a service is 'we Redis a list of naughty subnets and charge for the API key'—valuable plumbing, not a castle.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Create a minimal threat-rep feed: ingest public blocklists, score IPs by recency/volume, expose REST + bulk download, and wire a fail2ban or WAF rule sync script.

exhibitors.name: Acalvio Technologies
exhibitors.description: Acalvio is the leader in autonomous cyber deception, defending against security threats, including APTs, insider threats, and ransomware. Its AI-powered Preemptive Cybersecurity Platform delivers threat detection across IT, OT and cloud environments.
exhibitors.roast: Deception at scale—decoys, breadcrumbs, and lateral-movement theater—actually hurts to build well; the 'AI-powered' sticker is just expo seasoning.

exhibitors.name: Accedere
exhibitors.description: Accedere is a is presenting its GRC Compliance tool namely Controllo.ai . Controllo is an AI powered SaaS solution that closes faster for compliance obligations such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS, CMMC, FedRamp, Privacy etc mandates,
exhibitors.roast: Controllo.ai is compliance Mad Libs: paste your policies, get an AI paragraph that says you're ISO-shaped until the auditor asks for receipts.

exhibitors.name: AccuKnox
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor Secure All Assets - AI, Cloud, APIs, Data, Applications & SBOM Secure them Everywhere - Public, Private, Hybrid Cloud, Air-Gapped, Edge/IoT
exhibitors.roast: CSPM+CIEM+CASB+SBOM in one acronym burrito—under the hood it's API calls, JSON policies, and dashboards you could glue from open scanners and shame.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Glue a 'cloud posture' demo: CSPM-style checks with Prower/Steampipe or cloud APIs, IAM excess finder from IAM reports, and an SBOM from Syft in CI—one UI listing findings by service.

exhibitors.name: Act Security
exhibitors.description: Enterprises struggle to enforce least-privilege access - because "what can talk to what" is impossible to answer, and siloed identity and network controls leave the door open for attackers. Act Security safely untangles the access spaghetti by logically segmenting applications using native controls.
exhibitors.roast: Least-privilege spaghetti untangler sounds heroic until you realize it's 'use the cloud provider's actual groups and stop giving everyone Owner'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Script least-privilege baselines: export IAM bindings, flag overly broad roles, generate suggested bindings per app tier, and output Terraform or ClickOps steps to apply.

exhibitors.name: Action1
exhibitors.description: Action1 is an autonomous endpoint management platform trusted by many Fortune 500 companies. Cloud-native, infinitely scalable, highly secure, and configurable in 5 minutes—it just works and is free for the first 200 endpoints, with no limits.
exhibitors.roast: Cloud patch theater with a free tier hook—endpoint management is real work, but the moat is scripts and scale, not magic.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype patch orchestration: agent that runs OS update commands on a schedule, reports version inventory to a small API, and gates prod with maintenance windows—start with one OS.

exhibitors.name: ActiveState
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor ActiveState enables DevSecOps teams to improve their security posture while increasing productivity to deliver secure applications faster. Your one stop for secure, trusted, open source.
exhibitors.roast: Curated OSS builds and supply-chain peace of mind—worthy, but still SCA/dependency cosplay with a compiler and a support contract.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Set up a private PyPI/npm mirror with vulnerability scanning on upload (e.g. OSV-Scanner in CI) and lockfile-only installs—mimic 'trusted OSS' without the retainer.

exhibitors.name: Adaptiva
exhibitors.description: Adaptiva is the autonomous endpoint management company delivering the fastest, most scalable way to patch and manage devices. Enterprises rely on Adaptiva to boost IT efficiency, reduce endpoint security risk, and maximize patching velocity.
exhibitors.roast: Fast patching at scale is ops engineering, not wizardry—P2P delivery is neat, not a national secret.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use your config management (Ansible, Intune, or Munki) to push patches with staged rollout and health checks—add a simple web UI for rollout status.

exhibitors.name: Adaptive Security
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Adaptive Security is a next-gen cybersecurity platform protecting organizations from AI-powered threats through advanced training and phishing simulations, helping companies defend against deepfakes, GenAI phishing, and other emerging attacks.
exhibitors.roast: Phishing sims and deepfake panic videos—security awareness with extra fear; the tech is email templates and a video host, not a fortress.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship a phishing drill: landing pages, randomized send times, click tracking, and a leaderboard—use open phishing frameworks or a minimal Flask app with SMTP relay.

exhibitors.name: Aembit
exhibitors.description: Aembit is the IAM platform for agentic AI and workloads. It brings modern auth to non-human identities—enforcing identity- and context-based access via centralized policies to eliminate static credentials and reduce agent risk.
exhibitors.roast: Workload IAM for the AI era is OAuth for robots with a pricing page—important, but fundamentally policy + tokens + mTLS you can wire with SPIFFE-ish patterns.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Demo workload-to-workload auth: issue JWTs or mTLS certs via SPIRE or Step CA, enforce audience/scope in Envoy or API gateway, and log decisions to stdout for audit.

exhibitors.name: Agile Momentum
exhibitors.description: AgileMark delivers human behavioral risk deterrence. Starting where traditional DLP stops, phone camera snapping of screens. We now turn behavioral risk signals into targeted, bite-size interventions that correct risky behavior.
exhibitors.roast: DLP that watches people photograph monitors is 'HR analytics with a camera policy'—creepy, niche, and rebuildable with MDM and shame.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Pilot insider-risk signals without spyware: watermark sensitive docs, enable screen capture policies on managed devices, and correlate file exfil to DLP events in a single timeline.

exhibitors.name: AhnLab
exhibitors.description: Founded in 1995, AhnLab is a global cybersecurity leader providing comprehensive protection for endpoints, networks, and cloud environments. With advanced threat prevention and exceptional service, AhnLab secures over 25,000 organizations worldwide from evolving cyber threats.
exhibitors.roast: Old-guard APAC EDR with real kernel baggage and enterprise entrenchment—still, it's signatures and response playbooks racing against Microsoft Defender's budget.

exhibitors.name: AiStrike
exhibitors.description: AiStrike is an Agentic AI platform that unifies threat exposure, detection engineering, investigation, and response—automating and improving the full security operations lifecycle to proactively stop threats.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic SIEM is 'what if Splunk had delusions of autonomy'—orchestration glue with an LLM press release.

exhibitors.name: Aikido Security
exhibitors.description: Aikido is the no-nonsense security platform for developers. From code to cloud, we centralize scans, cut false positives, and automate fixes. Aikido streamlines security, speeds compliance, and helps teams scale. Automate security, don’t suffer it.
exhibitors.roast: Same SAST buffet, different carpet—two booths won't make CVE scanning less commoditized.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Consolidate findings: normalize SARIF from multiple scanners into one SQLite DB and a single dashboard with dedupe by file+rule—prove 'platform' is a join query.

exhibitors.name: Aikido Security
exhibitors.description: Aikido is the no-nonsense security platform for developers. From code to cloud, we centralize scans, cut false positives, and automate fixes. Aikido streamlines security, speeds compliance, and helps teams scale. Automate security, don’t suffer it.
exhibitors.roast: Same SAST buffet, different carpet—two booths won't make CVE scanning less commoditized.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Consolidate findings: normalize SARIF from multiple scanners into one SQLite DB and a single dashboard with dedupe by file+rule—prove 'platform' is a join query.

exhibitors.name: AirMDR
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor AirMDR offers an AI SOC platform (i.e., AI SOC Agent) and AI MDR service. Both combine agentic AI with human expertise to deliver minutes-fast alert investigations and transparent, audit-ready cases using the security stack customers already have.
exhibitors.roast: AI SOC that still bills like humans—strip the agentic sparkle and you're buying ticket hygiene with a chatbot forensics intern.

exhibitors.name: Airia
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Airia eliminates AI anxiety and accelerates adoption. We address the critical gap between agentic innovation and governance—empowering teams to build and deploy AI agents while maintaining control.
exhibitors.roast: You 'eliminate AI anxiety' by selling an AI governance platform -- which is itself AI. It's AI all the way down. Your product is a permission layer and audit log over LLM API calls, which is approximately what an API gateway with a logging middleware already does. Peak 2025 energy at a 2026 conference.

exhibitors.name: Akamai
exhibitors.description: Akamai is the cybersecurity and cloud computing company that powers and protects business online. Akamai Security provides market-leading solutions to secure your applications and networks using real-time intelligence and adaptive AI.
exhibitors.roast: You don't compete with their edge; you route through it—global anycast and peering drama is the definition of infra moat.

exhibitors.name: Akto
exhibitors.description: Akto is the Agentic AI Security platform for your teams to secure AI agents, MCPs, and LLMs in your organization. Akto helps in Discovery, Governance, red teaming, monitoring, and enforcing guardrails on Agentic AI assets.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic AI security for MCP and LLMs—if the pitch needs three acronyms and 'guardrails,' the product is mostly policy YAML and a model API.

exhibitors.name: AlgoSec
exhibitors.description: AlgoSec empowers 2,200+ global orgs to accelerate app delivery 10x faster with AI-driven automation, visibility, and security across hybrid networks. Learn more at www.algosec.com
exhibitors.roast: Firewall policy Tetris for enterprises—useful, but 'AI-driven' is doing a lot of brochure lifting while the core is still rules, paths, and change windows.

exhibitors.name: Alice
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Alice is a trust, safety, and security company built for the AI era, safeguarding communicative technologies across UGC and generative AI. Trusted by leading tech platforms and 7 of the 10 top foundation model labs, Alice delivers end-to-end coverage across the AI lifecycle - from red-teaming and model hardening to runtime guardrails and drift detection - powered by Rabbit Hole, our adversarial intelligence engine built on billions of real-world data samples and expert analy
exhibitors.roast: If your threat model is 'the model,' you are the model’s accessory brand—red team the frosting, harden the vibes, invoice the labs.

exhibitors.name: AlixPartners
exhibitors.description: AlixPartners is a results-driven global consulting firm that specializes in helping businesses successfully address their most complex and critical challenges.
exhibitors.roast: Not a product—it's billable adults in blazers shipping PowerPoint and subpoena energy. Your 'moat' is partners’ last names, not semver.

exhibitors.name: Amazon Web Services
exhibitors.description: AWS is the world's most comprehensive cloud platform, offering 240+ services globally. Millions of customers trust AWS to power infrastructure, increase agility, and lower costs.
exhibitors.roast: Atoms, electrons, regions, and an entire economy hooked on IAM footguns—destroying SaaS from the inside by selling the shovels.

exhibitors.name: American Portwell Technology, Inc.
exhibitors.description: American Portwell Technology, based in Fremont, CA, delivers reliable embedded systems and custom solutions for AI, network security, and more. With ISO-certified quality and Intel partnership, we drive innovation across diverse industries worldwide.
exhibitors.roast: Embedded boards and custom boxes—when the attack surface is a PCB, your CI/CD is a soldering iron and a purchase order.

exhibitors.name: Anomali
exhibitors.description: Anomali delivers an Agentic SOC platform underpinned by a unified security data lake, threat intelligence, analytics, and AI to improve detection, accelerate investigations, provide response plans, improve efficiency and scale security operations.
exhibitors.roast: Another 'unified lake' wearing a fedora: threat intel plus analytics plus 'agentic' is three tabs of Grafana and a Copilot macro.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: In a monorepo, add a small Python service with FastAPI that ingests STIX/TAXII or CSV IOCs into SQLite/Postgres, dedupes by (type,value,source), exposes /search and /export, and a daily job that scores freshness—ship a docker-compose and README with sample queries.

exhibitors.name: Anvilogic
exhibitors.description: Anvilogic is a security operations layer with AI-powered detection and triage. Its SIEM interface runs across SIEMs, data lakes, and hybrid environments, using detection content and SecOps agents to streamline and scale SOC operations.
exhibitors.roast: AI-powered triage across SIEMs is a fancy wrapper for 'parse alerts, cluster, draft note'—the vendors underneath still own you.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Write a Python worker that pulls alerts from a SIEM export (JSON lines), clusters by normalized fields + Jaccard on titles, assigns a severity score with rules + optional LLM summary behind a feature flag, writes tickets via REST, and stores outcomes for precision/recall.

exhibitors.name: AnyInsight.ai
exhibitors.description: AnyInsight.ai is a zero-trust, enterprise-grade GenAI platform with built-in agents, enabling secure and compliant AI deployment. It minimizes hallucinations, prevents data leakage and Shadow AI, and delivers reliable AI agents, without the need for heavy infrastructure or complex integrations.
exhibitors.roast: Zero-trust GenAI with 'built-in agents' is three words away from 'we fine-tuned procurement.'

exhibitors.name: Apiiro
exhibitors.description: Apiiro is the Agentic Application Security Platform, powered by the AutoFix Agent – force-multiplying application security and development teams to design, develop, and deliver secure software faster in the AI era.
exhibitors.roast: ASPM with an AutoFix agent is SCA cosplay with merge-request theater—still mostly dependency whack-a-mole.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Add GitHub Action: run OSV-Scanner + Semgrep on PRs, open a single summary comment with prioritized fixes, and a script that opens patch PRs bumping safe semver ranges using npm/pip tooling—fail the check only on critical reachable vulns.

exhibitors.name: Apono
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor Apono is a cloud-native privilege access management platform that eliminates standing privileges and enforces dynamic, runtime access control. Stop choosing between security and velocity. Apono makes privilege management intelligent and automatic.
exhibitors.roast: Dynamic least-privilege for clouds is IAM policy Sudoku—important, but it's policy JSON with a nicer haircut.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype a tiny policy engine: ingest cloud IAM JSON + resource graph from Terraform state, compute effective permissions for a role, and emit a 'standing privilege' report + suggested scoped policies with TTL fields.

exhibitors.name: AppGate
exhibitors.description: AppGate is the only direct-routed (no cloud bottlenecks) Zero Trust Network Access solution built for peak performance, superior protection and seamless interoperability, safeguarding complex environments, while reducing cost & increasing efficiency.
exhibitors.roast: ZTNA is SSH with marketing: split tunnels, identity, and a pricing page that scales with headcount.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up a minimal ZTNA demo: WireGuard or Tailscale ACLs + OIDC login + per-app DNS routes; document enrollment and revoke flows in one markdown runbook.

exhibitors.name: AppOmni
exhibitors.description: AppOmni prevents SaaS data breaches with end-to-end SaaS security, giving security teams posture, access, 3rd party connections, AI activity & unsanctioned SaaS visibility. Continuous monitoring & threat detection help identify & resolve risks early.
exhibitors.roast: SaaS posture is OAuth scopes and public links with anxiety—'AI activity visibility' is the new activity log export.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Create a SaaS posture scanner MVP: OAuth app inventory via IdP APIs + Slack/Google/Microsoft read-only scopes, flag risky scopes, external sharing, and inactive OAuth grants; output CSV + prioritized fixes.

exhibitors.name: AppSentinels Inc
exhibitors.description: AppSentinels is a unified API security platform that protects APIs throughout their lifecycle. By modeling APIs as workflows, it detects business logic abuse, data exposure, and AI-driven threats that bypass traditional security controls.
exhibitors.roast: API workflow graphs are pretty until someone ships a new route—then you're graphing yesterday's OpenAPI.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Generate OpenAPI from traffic (mitmproxy/har), diff routes between versions in CI, and run a small ruleset for authz gaps (missing auth on mutations, mass assignment patterns) with JSON SARIF output.

exhibitors.name: AppViewX
exhibitors.description: AppViewX is trusted by leading enterprises to prevent outages, reduce risk, and ensure compliance with automated CLM and PKI solutions. The platform delivers visibility, automation, and policy control while enabling crypto-agility.
exhibitors.roast: PKI/CLM still breaks because enterprises love 10-year roots and hate DNS—your moat is outage trauma, not AI.

exhibitors.name: Appdome
exhibitors.description: Appdome is the AI-Native platform for mobile fraud prevention and security, protecting mobile apps, APIs, and customer identity at runtime. Automate hundreds of defenses with no code, no SDKs, and seamless CI/CD integration.
exhibitors.roast: Mobile app shielding without SDK claims is either bytecode sleight-of-hand or lawyers—either way, not a weekend vibe-code.

exhibitors.name: Applivery
exhibitors.description: Applivery is a Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) platform that helps companies manage, secure, and automate Android, Apple, and Windows devices, giving IT teams full control, efficient app deployment, and compliance with strict security policies.
exhibitors.roast: UEM is MDM with international pavilion paella—policies, payloads, and praying Apple doesn’t change a plist.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a barebones MDM-ish control plane: enroll devices via enrollment profile URL, push a plist/mobileconfig for Wi-Fi/VPN, and a webhook receiver for inventory events—use sqlite for state and a single admin UI.

exhibitors.name: Apricorn
exhibitors.description: 256-Bit AES XTS Hardware-Encrypted USB External Data Storage. Cross-Platform Compatible, TAA Compliant, CMMC Level 1 Certified, FIPS 140-3 level 3 Compliant (cert pending), Designed, Assembled, Flashed and Packaged in San Diego California, USA.
exhibitors.roast: FIPS hardware in a USB stick—when the key is literal silicon, ChatGPT can't fork your supply chain.

exhibitors.name: Aqua Security
exhibitors.description: Aqua protects what runs in the cloud. As a pioneer in container security and vulnerability management, Aqua helps teams prioritize real risk, reduce vulnerability noise, and keep containerized applications secure and predictable across environments.
exhibitors.roast: Containers were the moat; now everyone has scanners and the real fight is noisy CVE prioritization and Kubernetes drama.

exhibitors.name: Arcanna AI
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor At Arcanna, we are dedicated to revolutionizing cybersecurity with a unique blend of human expertise and state-of-the-art artificial intelligence technology. The platform operates in any mission-critical environment, from SOC to NOC and beyond.
exhibitors.roast: Human expertise plus AI in SOAR is 'analyst plus autocomplete'—the playbooks were YAML before they were trendy.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Implement a minimal SOAR: webhook ingests alert JSON, maps to a playbook YAML (if crowdstrike then slack+ticket), executes steps with retries/idempotency keys, stores run history in Postgres.

exhibitors.name: Arch0
exhibitors.description: We're not in the business of alerts. We're in the business of answers. Arch0 is an intelligence layer built on a living knowledge graph of your organization. A swarm of specialized agents evaluate every signal by real business impact, auto-remediate, and loop you in only when it matters.
exhibitors.roast: Living knowledge graph plus specialized agents is startup speak for 'we glued Postgres, vectors, and optimism.'

exhibitors.name: Archer
exhibitors.description: Archer delivers integrated risk management solutions that help enterprises manage risk, ensure compliance, and adapt to change. Using AI and risk quantification, we provide insights that drive strategy and resilience. Visit www.ArcherIRM.com.
exhibitors.roast: Integrated risk management is spreadsheets with audit trail and a login page—AI risk quantification is still Monte Carlo with a logo.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a lightweight risk register: CSV import, control mapping to frameworks (NIST/ISO) as tags, simple Monte Carlo on impact/frequency columns, export board slide markdown.

exhibitors.name: Arctic Wolf Den
exhibitors.description: Experience the Arctic Wolf Aurora Platform at RSAC™ 2026—visit the Wolf Den at the corner of 4th and Howard! Join us to discover a higher standard of AI-driven security operations, featuring enhanced visibility and continuous threat monitoring with unmatched power and scale.
exhibitors.roast: Sponsored experience with 'AI-driven ops' is a lounge that smells like espresso and recurring revenue.

exhibitors.name: Arctic Wolf Networks
exhibitors.description: Arctic Wolf® is a global leader in security operations, delivering the first cloud-native security operations platform to end cyber risk, providing 24×7 monitoring, detection, response, and risk management.
exhibitors.roast: 24x7 SOC-as-a-service scales with humans in shifts—software helps, payroll is the product.

exhibitors.name: Armis
exhibitors.description: Armis, the cyber exposure management & security company, secures large enterprises, governments, critical infrastructure, economies, and society at scale by providing visibility, security, and control of all physical and virtual assets (IT, OT, IoT, medical devices) from the ground to the cloud.
exhibitors.roast: Asset inventory across IT/OT/IoT is genuinely ugly telemetry—your moat is parsers, not prompts.

exhibitors.name: Astrix Security
exhibitors.description: Astrix enables enterprises to discover every AI agent and NHI, secure excessive privileges and real-time threats, and deploy agentic AI safely with secure-by-design guardrails—allowing organizations to adopt AI securely and at scale.
exhibitors.roast: Non-human identity for AI agents is IAM with extra nouns—still OAuth, roles, and panic about tokens.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build an NHI inventory: list service accounts + OAuth apps from cloud IdP, map to owners, detect unused keys/credentials older than 90 days, output remediation tickets with suggested rotation steps.

exhibitors.name: Attack of the AI Sprawl | Experience the Film with Reco
exhibitors.description: Lights. Games. Action. Transform into your AI alter ego, play arcade-style challenges, and immerse yourself in a cinematic mission to stop AI sprawl—powered by Reco’s visibility into apps, agents, and SaaS risk.
exhibitors.roast: A sponsored 'cinematic mission' to stop AI sprawl—powered by Reco, which is itself an AI platform. The snake eating its own tail, but with arcade games.

exhibitors.name: Aurascape
exhibitors.description: Aurascape is the AI security platform that helps enterprises see, govern, and coach every element of AI interactions. Intent- and entitlement-aware controls, in-flow coaching, and audit-ready evidence at scale give you the control to safely adopt AI.
exhibitors.roast: ‘Intent-aware’ is what you call policy when your differentiator is an LLM reading Slack and calling it governance.

exhibitors.name: Aurva
exhibitors.description: Aurva is the runtime security platform for AI agents. We monitor agentic data access, detect behavioral anomalies, and enforce compliance controls in real time. Purpose-built for the agentic AI era, Aurva delivers visibility where network security goes blind.
exhibitors.roast: Agent runtime monitoring is real work, but you’re one Grafana dashboard away from twelve seed-stage clones with the same ‘anomaly’ slide.

exhibitors.name: AuthLite
exhibitors.description: AuthLite is multi-factor Authentication (MFA, 2FA) for Active Directory admins and users, including network access. AuthLite assigns privileged groups dynamically after login, and eliminates credential exposure risks like Pass-the-Hash attacks.
exhibitors.roast: MFA for Active Directory is essential—and also something a motivated admin scripts between coffee runs.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Implement Windows Hello / FIDO2 for privileged AD groups only: document current Group Policy objects, add conditional access-equivalent controls via GPO + certificate mapping, and prove Pass-the-Hash fails in a lab DC with before/after BloodHound exports.

exhibitors.name: Avertro
exhibitors.description: CyberHQ® is the Resilience Command Platform that directs your defense. We translate technical signals into quantifiable, governance-ready intelligence, empowering you to validate cyber effectiveness, prove defensible resilience, and optimize security-per-dollar with absolute confidence.
exhibitors.roast: CyberHQ Resilience Command Platform sounds like a PowerBI board that learned to spell ‘NIST’.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace the GRC dashboard with code: export one control framework (e.g. ISO 27001 Annex A) into YAML, map each control to Terraform modules + CI gates, and fail builds when evidence artifacts are missing—no BI tool in the loop.

exhibitors.name: Axiomtek
exhibitors.description: Axiomtek is a global leader in industrial computing and edge AI solutions, delivering secure, reliable hardware platforms for networking, cybersecurity, IoT, and mission-critical applications. Our systems power worldwide intelligent infrastructures.
exhibitors.roast: Industrial PCs age like cast iron: boring until the plant stops because someone ‘secured’ the wrong serial port.

exhibitors.name: AxisNow
exhibitors.description: AxisNow is a cloud-agnostic edge platform that allows easily deployment of private and multiple CDNs. By decoupling edge infrastructure from edge services, the way edge services are used and purchased will undergo fundamental changes.
exhibitors.roast: Multi-CDN edge orchestration is legit—until your pricing slide becomes indistinguishable from Cloudflare’s footnote.

exhibitors.name: Axoflow
exhibitors.description: Axoflow is the Security Data Layer that sits under your SIEM/analytics tools, enabling the use of AI, up to 70% faster investigations, and more than 50% reduction in SIEM spend by feeding them with actionable data. From the creators of syslog-ng.
exhibitors.roast: syslog-ng pedigree buys you meetings; the rest is ‘we make SIEM cheaper’ in a nicer font.

exhibitors.name: Axonius
exhibitors.description: Axonius is the leading asset intelligence and exposure management platform. By connecting with 1,300+ tools, we give security teams a unified view of all assets and exposures: uncovering gaps and automating remediation to reduce risk.
exhibitors.roast: 1,300 connectors is a moat of paperwork: impressive until the next vendor promises 1,301 and a fresher UI.

exhibitors.name: Axur
exhibitors.description: Axur protects brands and data across the digital landscape. We monitor cyber threats, fight online fraud, detect data leaks, and remove malicious content—delivering comprehensive digital security for companies that value their reputation.
exhibitors.roast: Brand intel is a data-hoarding business; your graph is the product, not the dashboard chrome.

exhibitors.name: BASE4 Security
exhibitors.description: We are a cybersecurity consulting firm with a presence in the Americas and Europe, specializing in supporting organizations that seek to protect their business proactively and sustainably.
exhibitors.roast: Consulting with an ASM sticker: you’re buying hours with a logo that says ‘we also scan’.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run open-source recon (subfinder, httpx, nuclei) against one approved domain, store results in Git, and produce a one-page risk report with exploitable vs informational—billable narrative optional.

exhibitors.name: BSI Group
exhibitors.description: For more than 120 years, BSI has shaped the global best practices and provided training, assessment, and certification. In the digital trust sector, our key focus areas include cybersecurity, data integrity, AI, and digital supply chain management.
exhibitors.roast: Standards and certification: a moat made of paperwork that outlasts most startups’ entire cap table.

exhibitors.name: Backline
exhibitors.description: Backline is the first vulnerability management platform, scaled to fix. We go beyond tickets and queues to deliver safe, autonomous remediation. For us, remediation isn’t triage, it’s the actual work of fixing, through a preemptive and continuous process that reduces backlog, MTTR, and risk.
exhibitors.roast: Autonomous remediation is ‘close the ticket’ with a cron job and better liability insurance.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Pick the top 10 CVEs from your ASM export, write idempotent patch playbooks (Terraform/Ansible), wire them to a manual approval gate in CI, and log rollback steps—prove mean time to patch drops without a new SaaS SKU.

exhibitors.name: Backslash Security
exhibitors.description: Backslash is the vibe coding security company. Our platform is purpose-built to empower organizations to accelerate their use of AI-native software development and vibe coding safely and securely, leveraging the native capabilities of tools such as Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub CoPilot, and Gemini.
exhibitors.roast: They literally branded ‘vibe coding security’—the booth is a mirror with a linter taped to it.

exhibitors.name: Barndoor AI
exhibitors.description: Barndoor AI is the AI governance platform built for the agentic enterprise. Barndoor's centralized control plane gives IT and security teams the visibility and fine-grained access control they need to govern AI agents across every tool, team, and platform.
exhibitors.roast: Closing the barn door after the agent exfiltrated the database, but make it a ‘control plane’.

exhibitors.name: Beacon Security
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Beacon is the security data management platform that enables teams to easily collect, reduce, enrich, and route their security data – so analysts and AI agents have the context they need to detect and stop threats faster.
exhibitors.roast: Collect, reduce, enrich, route—aka syslog with a LinkedIn Premium account.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace the pipeline PoC with Vector or Fluent Bit configs: define one schema, drop noisy events at ingest, route security logs to cold storage with retention policies, and benchmark monthly egress cost vs current SIEM.

exhibitors.name: BeyGoo
exhibitors.description: BeyGoo is the first Extended Digital Risk Protection (DRPX) platform. An evolved concept that goes beyond the detection and remediation of digital risks, integrating cyber intelligence, specialized analysts, and AI within a single ecosystem.
exhibitors.roast: Extended Digital Risk Protection is BrandShield’s cousin who studied abroad and came back with three extra acronyms.

exhibitors.name: BeyondTrust
exhibitors.description: BeyondTrust is the global cybersecurity leader protecting Paths to Privilege™ with an identity-centric approach. We are leading the charge in transforming identity security and are trusted by 20,000 customers, including 75 of the Fortune 100.
exhibitors.roast: PAM for 20,000 customers is enterprise gravity, not physics—break-glass still breaks the budget.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Implement least-privilege sessions for break-glass: time-bound elevation, session recording to immutable storage, automatic revocation, and quarterly access reviews exported as CSV from IdP + PAM logs.

exhibitors.name: BigID
exhibitors.description: BigID helps organizations connect data & AI for security, privacy, compliance, & AI governance, enabling them to discover, understand,protect, & act on high-risk,high-value data across cloud & on-prem environments to reduce risk & automate compliance
exhibitors.roast: Data classification plus ‘AI governance’ is compliance Mad Libs with a Salesforce renewal attached.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Classify PII in one datastore with deterministic rules first (regex + column metadata), publish classification tags to your catalog API, and block queries in the warehouse role layer when tags don’t match—LLM optional, evidence mandatory.

exhibitors.name: Bindplane
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Bindplane unifies your security data, accelerates SIEM migrations, and can route logs to low-cost storage — reducing costs and eliminating vendor lock-in with a scalable telemetry pipeline.
exhibitors.roast: SIEM migration glue: the tool you buy when your Splunk bill qualifies for its own line item on the earnings call.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype SIEM offload: sample 1% of high-volume logs to object storage, replay queries with Athena/Trino, and document query latency + cost per GB—prove the migration math before you pay for routing middleware.

exhibitors.name: Bitdefender
exhibitors.description: Bitdefender is a cybersecurity leader delivering best‑in‑class threat prevention, detection, and response. Protecting millions of users worldwide, Bitdefender is a trusted expert in eliminating threats and enabling cyber resilience.
exhibitors.roast: Endpoint AV is a shrinking moat, but it’s still a moat full of angry cats and kernel callbacks.

exhibitors.name: Bitwarden
exhibitors.description: Bitwarden empowers enterprises, developers, and individuals to securely manage identities and share sensitive information online with trusted security solutions for passwords, secrets, and passkey management.
exhibitors.roast: Password managers are critical infrastructure you can also fork on a weekend if you hate sleep.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Roll out passkeys for admin accounts only: inventory OIDC/SAML apps, enforce WebAuthn in IdP policy, and measure support tickets weekly—ship a rollback plan before you touch general users.

exhibitors.name: Black Duck
exhibitors.description: Black Duck meets the board-level risks of modern software with True Scale Application Security, ensuring uncompromised trust in software for the regulated, AI-powered world.
exhibitors.roast: True Scale Application Security is enterprise-speak for ‘we run SCA until your pipeline files for unemployment’.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Add SBOM generation (Syft) + policy gate in CI blocking high CVEs in direct deps; fail the build on unapproved licenses; store CycloneDX in an OCI artifact—no ‘AI-powered’ triage, just semver and CVE IDs.

exhibitors.name: Blast
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor Blast is the first Preemptive Cloud Defense platform, turning native cloud controls into preventive guardrails that eliminate risk at the source and reduce blast radius. We replace reactive security with continuous prevention, safely and at scale.
exhibitors.roast: Preemptive Cloud Defense is CSPM with a pep talk and a prettier deny policy.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Encode preventive guardrails as Terraform/OpenAPI policy: SCPs, org-level KMS defaults, and automated remediation PRs for public S3/RDS—score compliance with prowler or equivalent weekly reports.

exhibitors.name: BlinkOps
exhibitors.description: BlinkOps is an Agentic Security Automation Platform where AI-driven agents manage alerts, investigations, and containment. Built via no-code or LLM tools, agents run safely on Blink’s engine, scaling security with speed and precision.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic SOAR is playbooks with chat bubbles: the automation was always there; the invoice is new.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace ‘AI agents’ with deterministic runbooks: webhook → Jira ticket → isolate host via EDR API → Slack thread with evidence links; measure MTTR for 20 simulated alerts; keep humans on approve for containment.

exhibitors.name: Blumira
exhibitors.description: Blumira simplifies security operations, making enterprise-grade protection accessible to every organization. With pre-built detections, 24/7 support, and expert guidance paired with intelligent automation, we help teams find and stop threats fast.
exhibitors.roast: SIEM-in-a-box for teams that outgrew Excel but not vendor webinars.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up detections as code: Sigma rules in Git, CI validation, deploy to your log stack, and tune with false-positive metrics per rule—prove value before expanding log sources.

exhibitors.name: Booz Allen
exhibitors.description: Booz Allen is an advanced technology company building AI, cyber, and other cutting-edge tech solutions to fast-track results for government and safeguard America.
exhibitors.roast: The booth carpet is classified; the swag is compliance-budgeted.

exhibitors.name: BotCity
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor BotCity is a platform focused on Python and Agents governance. It monitors the use of Python+AI in official endpoints and pipelines, delivers reliable inventory, audit trails, and risk insights to mitigate Shadow Python and strengthen security.
exhibitors.roast: You invented the term 'Shadow Python' so you could sell a solution to a problem nobody was Googling. Your product is `pip list` with a compliance dashboard. The RSA booth fee probably cost more than it would take to rebuild this as a pre-commit hook.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a Python runtime governance tool. CLI agent that scans environments for Python installations and virtual environments, runs `pip list --format=json` to build inventory, compares against an allowlist policy (YAML config). Ship a Next.js dashboard showing inventory per host, flags policy violations, tracks drift over time. Add a GitHub Action blocking PRs adding unapproved dependencies.

exhibitors.name: BrainStorm Inc.
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor BrainStorm reduces end-user security risk through Security Awareness and Threat Defense Packs that combine proactive education with remedial training. The platform makes makes behavior change measurable and repeatable.
exhibitors.roast: Threat defense packs: PowerPoint DLC for companies that measure culture via completion percentages.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace generic training with role-based scenarios: developers get secret scanning + dependency PR labs; finance gets wire-fraud simulations; track outcomes with phishing simulations tied to each module.

exhibitors.name: BrandShield
exhibitors.description: BrandShield provides an AI Powered Digital Risk Protection solution. Our technology proactively uncovers and eliminates cyber risks such as phishing attacks, social media scams, impersonation, paid ad scams, counterfeiting, and other digital risks.
exhibitors.roast: AI-powered digital risk protection is ‘grep the internet for your logo’ with a margin.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a minimal typosquat monitor: daily cert transparency + DNS crawl for your brand strings, alert on new registrations, and auto-file takedowns via registrar abuse APIs where supported.

exhibitors.name: BreachLock
exhibitors.description: BreachLock is a global leader in offensive security offering human-led, AI-augmented Penetration Testing as a Service (PTaaS), agentic AI-powered Adversarial Exposure Validation (AEV), & automated, CTEM-aligned solutions within one unified platform.
exhibitors.roast: Human-led, AI-augmented pentest: still a human clicking, now with a summarizer for the PDF.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run a scoped PTaaS-style assessment internally: OWASP ASVS checklist, store findings in Git issues with repro steps, and gate releases on critical/high remediation with retest evidence.

exhibitors.name: Bright Security
exhibitors.description: Bright Security is transforming AppSec for the AI era. Our AI-powered STAR platform embeds security into development, automating detection, remediation, and validation - empowering teams to innovate rapidly with security seamlessly built in.
exhibitors.roast: STAR platform with SAST/DAST is a constellation of tools your intern could wire with GitHub Actions and spite.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Integrate Semgrep + ZAP in CI for one service: block on OWASP Top 10 classes, publish SARIF to GitHub Security tab, and require fix PRs before merge on default branch.

exhibitors.name: Brinqa
exhibitors.description: Security teams are drowning in data, alerts, and tools. Brinqa helps teams cut through the chaos in a smart way, with exposure management that delivers clarity, prioritization, and confidence, turning complex signals into decisions they can trust.
exhibitors.roast: Exposure management with confidence is vuln prioritization for people who want confidence spelled with a trademark.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Unify findings from one scanner into a single risk score: EPSS + asset business criticality in a spreadsheet or DB, review weekly, and auto-create tickets only above threshold—skip the black-box ‘AI prioritization’.

exhibitors.name: Broadcom
exhibitors.description: Symantec and Carbon Black's combined portfolios deliver defenses, designed to respond to today’s enterprise-grade threats while delivering stronger and more adaptive security tomorrow.
exhibitors.roast: Symantec plus Carbon Black: enterprise inertia incarnate, held together by licensing lawyers.

exhibitors.name: Build Your Own Agent with Databricks
exhibitors.description: Build your own Databricks Agent at our Minifig Bar, or talk Data + Security at our community lego table. Let's see what we can build together!
exhibitors.roast: Security SKU on a lakehouse: powerful, still mostly ‘please buy compute’ with compliance stickers.

exhibitors.name: Bureau Veritas
exhibitors.description: Bureau Veritas Cybersecurity helps organizations reduce risk and strengthen defenses while meeting industry standards. Our services span people, processes, and technology, from training and social engineering to security consulting and pentesting.
exhibitors.roast: Pentest-as-a-service from a company that also certifies your toaster’s feelings.

exhibitors.name: CHANGING Information Technology Inc.
exhibitors.description: CHANGING (TPEX:8272), established in 1998 as a professional information security company under Wistron Corporation, focuses on "information security" and "authentication of People, Things, and IoT" as its core.

exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Consolidate MFA policies: FIDO2 for workforce, TOTP fallback with device binding, disable SMS for privileged roles, export monthly auth failure metrics from IdP logs.

exhibitors.name: CHT Security
exhibitors.description: CHT Security (TWSE: 7765) is a specialized cybersecurity subsidiary of Chunghwa Telecom, the largest telco, ISP, and one of the most trustworthy ICT companies. Being the leading MSSP in APAC, delivers comprehensive cyber security services.

exhibitors.name: CLEAR
exhibitors.description: The CLEAR Cafe is a lounge activation for conference attendees to rest, recharge, and grab a coffee!
exhibitors.roast: Biometric IAM lounge: skip the line, not the privacy policy.

exhibitors.name: CYBERANI
exhibitors.description: Protecting Cyberspace At Cyberani, we are dedicated to safeguarding the digital frontiers of industry leaders and government agencies. Founded with the vision to set the standard for cyber resilience, our mission is crystal clear: to ensure steady protection for our client assets and operations with next-generation preventive and responsive cybersecurity services. We are committed to delivering next-generation cybersecurity services that ensure steady protection and regulatory compliance for ind
exhibitors.roast: National-brand MSSP cosplay: impressive flag, still tickets and playbooks underneath.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Productize SLAs, ticketing, runbooks, and monthly PDFs—honest services beat fake platforms.

exhibitors.name: CYE
exhibitors.description: Cye combines an AI-native exposure management platform with expert augmentation to help organizations understand true exposure and prioritize ROI-led mitigation. With financial clarity and partnership, Cye enables confident cyber-risk decisions.

exhibitors.name: CYJAX
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor CYJAX is a cyber threat intelligence company that turns complex threat data into clear, actionable insight. Combining advanced technology with expert analysis, it helps organisations detect, understand, and respond to cyber risk faster.

exhibitors.name: Capital One Software Coffee Barcade
exhibitors.description: Visit booth N-4700 to experience our: • Capital One Databolt Demos: See how Databolt protects your sensitive data for AI workloads. • Espresso Bar: Enjoy drinks throughout the show. • AI Trading Cards: Visit our AI photobooth to create your own collectible trading card. • Claw Machine: Try your chance to secure one of our custom plushies.
exhibitors.roast: Enterprise B2B from a bank: either real internal battle scars or very expensive PowerPoint lineage.

exhibitors.description: Autonomous Security Testing for your web apps, APIs, infrastructure, and AI systems. Human supervision optionally available.

Add a cron job that re-checks domains every 24h and diffs results. exhibitors.roast: You raised $1B+ to grep node_modules for CVEs. Your SAST is pattern-matching ASTs, your SCA is a lookup table against public vulnerability databases, and now you slapped 'AI Trust Platform' on the homepage because the VCs need an exit story. Claude already understands code semantics better than your rules engine ever will.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a developer security scanning platform. Tech stack: TypeScript CLI + Next.js dashboard + Postgres. For SCA: parse lockfiles (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, requirements.txt, go.sum, Cargo.lock) to build a dependency tree, then query the free OSV.dev API for each package/version to get known vulns. For SAST: use tree-sitter to parse source code into ASTs for JS/TS/Python/Go/Java, then write detection rules for OWASP Top 10 patterns. For container scanning: parse Dockerfiles, extract base image, run SCA on OS packages inside image layers. For IaC: parse Terraform/CloudFormation as HCL/JSON/YAML, check against CIS benchmark rules. Wire into a GitHub App that comments on PRs with findings. Use OpenAI API for human-readable fix suggestions. Store results in Postgres, dashboard shows trends over time per repo.
