exhibitors.name: Straiker
exhibitors.description: Straiker delivers real-time protection for agentic AI applications using proprietary models trained by its AI Security Research Team. Founded by AI and cybersecurity veterans, backed by top-tier investors, Straiker reasons across every layer to ensure complete security.
exhibitors.roast: 'Proprietary models' protecting agents is a matryoshka doll of models watching models argue about policy.

exhibitors.name: Stream.Security
exhibitors.description: Stream.Security is an AI Detection and Response platform purpose-built for the cloud. Stream's CloudTwin™, a live, stateful model of your cloud, powers accurate, risk-based detection, AI triage, and impact-aware response.
exhibitors.roast: CloudTwin sounds like a Sims expansion pack for your misconfigured S3 bucket.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype a read-only cloud asset inventory (IAM, buckets, SG rules) with drift alerts; call it a 'twin' only if it replays events.

exhibitors.name: Strike
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Strike is a CTEM platform that runs continuous Threat Emulations over your attack surface to detect anomalies before they can get vulnerated.
exhibitors.roast: CTEM is BAS with a LinkedIn rebrand; DAST in the name means you brought a scanner to a policy fight.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Wrap OWASP ZAP or Nuclei in CI with SARIF + ticket creation; skip the acronym soup pricing page.

exhibitors.name: StrongKeep
exhibitors.description: StrongKeep reimagines cybersecurity for SMBs without security teams. Our all-in-one platform bundles endpoint protection, employee training, and guided compliance so businesses can meet requirements quickly without enterprise complexity.
exhibitors.roast: All-in-one SMB security is antivirus, a PDF checklist, and a calendar reminder labeled 'compliance'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship a single-tenant SMB bundle: EDR agent stub, phishing sim CSV import, policy doc generator from a YAML template.

exhibitors.name: StrongestLayer
exhibitors.description: StrongestLayer stops AI-generated threats legacy solutions miss. Our email security platform uses LLM-native detection for novel attacks while empowering employees with in-inbox coaching. Deploy in minutes via API with minimal false positives.
exhibitors.roast: LLM-native email security is spam filters cosplaying as philosophy majors.

exhibitors.name: Sublime Security
exhibitors.description: Sublime stops more email attacks with less work. Our agentic platform protects, adapts, and responds in real-time—without vendor bottlenecks.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic email defense is rules plus retries with an intern's confidence interval.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Parse inbound mail via API, run YARA-like rules + SPF/DKIM/DMARC checks, post to Slack; measure precision on a labeled set.

exhibitors.name: Sumo Logic
exhibitors.description: Sumo Logic makes the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by turning insights into action.
exhibitors.roast: Another log haystack with a pricing model designed to fund your CFO's therapy.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up OpenSearch + vector-free alerting on JSON logs; prove ingest $/GB vs. your renewal.

exhibitors.name: SurePath AI
exhibitors.description: SurePath AI is a SaaS platform that governs any GenAI solutions you build, adopt, or buy, even Shadow AI. It secures both public and private models, detects usage, mitigates risks, and controls data access—all within your existing security framework.
exhibitors.roast: GenAI governance is HR for robots: lots of forms, little enforcement.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a proxy that logs prompts/responses, blocks PII via regex+NER, and exports an audit CSV—ship before 'policy engine' fantasies.

exhibitors.name: Swimlane
exhibitors.description: The leader in agentic AI automation for every security function.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic SOAR is playbooks with extra syllables and the same brittle API keys.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use n8n or Temporal to chain three SOAR actions with retries/idempotency; compare runbook time to vendor demo.

exhibitors.name: Swissbit
exhibitors.description: Swissbit is shaping a world where data and identities can be trusted, enabling true digital sovereignty. With hardware-based security products for authentication (FIDO), data and device protection, Swissbit empowers zero-trust architectures.
exhibitors.roast: You make actual silicon in actual factories in actual Switzerland. Your biggest risk isn't an AI startup -- it's a tariff negotiation. The only thing a vibe coder can do to compete with you is order your chips on Mouser and put them in a worse enclosure.

exhibitors.name: Sygnia
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Sygnia is the foremost global incident response and cyber readiness team, applying creative approaches and battle-tested solutions to help organizations beat attackers and stay secure, continuously.
exhibitors.roast: Elite IR is a moat of sleep deprivation and NDAs, not a scalable SaaS margin.

exhibitors.name: Symphion, Inc.
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Symphion is the world leader in print fleet cyber hygiene, protecting real world fleets from over 30,000 down to 300 printers. Our Print Fleet Cyber Security as a Service™ (PFCSAAS), protects printer endpoints across all makes, models, and firmware.
exhibitors.roast: Printer security is real and deeply unsexy—like dental work for VLANs.

exhibitors.name: Synqly
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor Synqly, The Connected Platform for Security, IT, and AI, streamlines integrations for OEMs and enterprises with data orchestration and normalization to ensure scalable, secure connectivity.
exhibitors.roast: Normalization middleware: the industry's favorite way to delay fixing the actual schema.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Write adapters that map three vendor alert JSONs to OCSF-ish records; open-source the transforms, sell support if you must.

exhibitors.name: Syteca
exhibitors.description: Syteca is redefining how organizations secure privileged access by unifying granular PAM, full session visibility, and built-in Identity Threat Detection and Response (ITDR) into one lightweight solution.
exhibitors.roast: Unified PAM is SSH with a receipt printer and a billing department.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype session recording for SSH/RDP with command allowlists and break-glass workflow in a week.

exhibitors.name: Tailscale
exhibitors.description: The secure connectivity platform for modern development teams
exhibitors.roast: WireGuard in a nice coat with SSO—hard to displace because networks are sticky, not because the idea is novel.

exhibitors.name: TeamT5
exhibitors.description: Established in Taiwan, we harness our geographical and cultural strengths to deliver unparalleled Asia Pacific intelligence. Our unique intelligence-driven solutions empower enterprises to strengthen cybersecurity against threats with precision.
exhibitors.roast: Regional intel is a moat until your feed overlaps everyone else's blog a week later.

exhibitors.name: Tego AI
exhibitors.description: Tego AI is where autonomy meets security. We secure AI agents by verifying identity and understanding purpose and intent, enabling safe autonomy through precise, just-in-time access to organizational resources, while ensuring full visibility and control over every agent and every action.
exhibitors.roast: Intent for agents is vibes lint for chatbots—useful theater until the tool calls anyway.

exhibitors.name: Teleport
exhibitors.description: Teleport, the AI Infrastructure Identity Company, establishes a unified identity layer for infrastructure — humans, machines, workloads, and AI agents — secured cryptographically.
exhibitors.roast: Infra identity is unsexy plumbing that breaks catastrophically when done wrong—so incumbency matters.

exhibitors.name: Tenable
exhibitors.description: Tenable® is the exposure management company, exposing and closing the cybersecurity gaps that erode business value, reputation and trust. Tenable reduces business risk for over 40,000 customers around the globe. Learn more at tenable.com.
exhibitors.roast: Exposure management is vuln scanning with a LinkedIn glow-up and a renewal calendar.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run Trivy/Grype in CI + OSQuery for inventory; export to CSV; compare coverage to your Nessus bill.

exhibitors.name: Teramind
exhibitors.description: As a global leader in user behavior analytics, insider risk management, and workforce intelligence, Teramind empowers businesses with complete visibility across digital environments. Teramind's Predictive Edge, powered by brAIn, delivers AI-driven insights that proactively prevent insider threats, ensure compliance, and enhance performance while respecting privacy. Trusted by thousands of organizations worldwide, Teramind helps businesses operate with clarity and thrive in a digital world.
exhibitors.roast: Insider risk analytics: keylogging's MBA cousin with a 'predictive edge' sticker.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: If you need DLP-lite, start with endpoint file exfil rules + USB policy via MDM; measure HR complaints before full UBA.

exhibitors.name: TestifySec
exhibitors.description: TestifySec — AI-powered compliance automation. Co-authors of NIST SP 800-204D and creators of witness.dev, the industry-standard attestation framework.
exhibitors.roast: AI compliance automation is checklists that learned to autocomplete.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Map controls to evidence folders with git commits as proof; generate auditor packet ZIP from markdown—skip the agent swarm.

exhibitors.name: Thales
exhibitors.description: Thales, a global leader in cybersecurity, helps organizations protect critical applications, data, identities, and software. Security for what matters most.
exhibitors.roast: When your product needs a truck and a tamper mesh, you're not in 'SaaS,' you're in physics.

exhibitors.name: The Optro Launchpad
exhibitors.description: Optro’s Launchpad is ready to take you to the Connected Risk Universe! Blast the asteroids in our game, Risk Universe Run. Then collect your pin and bring it to the Optro Space Station (Booth #S-1749) to complete your flight kit.
exhibitors.roast: A flight kit for a conference hall: marketing calories, zero CVEs.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Not applicable—promo booth; spend the time on sleep, not code.

exhibitors.name: Thinkst Canary
exhibitors.description: Most companies discover they've been breached way too late. Thinkst Canary fixes this: just 2 minutes of setup; no ongoing overhead; nearly 0 false positives, and you can detect attackers long before they dig in.
exhibitors.roast: Deception so simple it hurts competitors who sell 'AI-driven honeypots' for 40x the price.

exhibitors.name: Thoropass™
exhibitors.description: Thoropass provides the most thorough audit prep, continuous monitoring, and security audit experience through easy software and expert guidance – so you can do business with confidence. Learn more at www.thoropass.com.
exhibitors.roast: Audit prep SaaS is anxiety-as-a-service with SOC 2 seasoning.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Automate evidence collection: pull SSO logs, ticket closures, access reviews into dated folders; diff month-over-month.

exhibitors.name: ThreatBreaker
exhibitors.description: ThreatBreaker is an AI-native endpoint security platform for MSSPs and SMBs, focused on automated forensics, incident prevention, and data exfiltration detection. We help security teams respond faster and operate at scale with less manual effort.
exhibitors.roast: AI-native EDR for SMBs is 'we slapped GPT on Sysmon and called it tier-1 replacement'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship Sigma rules + Velociraptor queries for ten TTPs; measure MTTR on a lab domain before ML.

exhibitors.name: ThreatConnect
exhibitors.description: ThreatConnect, now part of Dataminr, provides solutions to enable cyber defenders to continuously manage threat exposure and improve cyber resilience. Our products span threat, risk, and SecOps, and come together in a single intelligence hub.
exhibitors.roast: Threat intel platforms age like sushi: fine at purchase, questionable by afternoon keynote.

exhibitors.name: ThreatLocker
exhibitors.description: Stop attacks before they happen. ThreatLocker brings Zero Trust solutions to your organization with controls that put you in charge of who - and - what gets into your environment.
exhibitors.roast: Zero trust endpoint allowlisting is powerful and also why your helpdesk now hates you.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Prototype AppLocker/WDAC policies + ring rollout; log denials to SIEM before buying another 'zero trust' panel.

exhibitors.name: ThreatModeler
exhibitors.description: ThreatModeler is the innovation leader in intelligent and automated threat modeling, helping enterprises build secure applications, devices, and cloud infrastructure by design.
exhibitors.roast: Automated threat modeling is STRIDE with autocomplete and a Jira integration nobody asked for.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use OWASP Threat Dragon on three services; export diagrams; automate only the boring duplicate edges.

exhibitors.name: Threatcop Inc.
exhibitors.description: Threatcop helps organizations transform employees from the weakest link into the strongest defense with People Security Management platform. Powered by the AAPE framework (Assess, Aware, Protect, Empower), we provide real-time threat detection, protection, and gamified training to reduce human risk.
exhibitors.roast: AAPE sounds like a framework for selling phishing sims to people who already failed the quiz.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run monthly phishing tests with one landing page and track repeat clickers; publish a leaderboard nobody wants.

exhibitors.name: Tidal Cyber
exhibitors.description: The Tidal Cyber Threat-Led Defense platform ensures your security stack is equipped to defend against relevant threats by mapping your tools against TTPs to reveal where they effectively defend against adversary behaviors and pinpoint coverage gaps.
exhibitors.roast: Mapping tools to TTPs is useful until your map is just MITRE fanfiction with a subscription.

exhibitors.name: Tines
exhibitors.description: Tines is an intelligent workflow platform that powers the world’s most important workflows. IT and security teams at companies like Canva, Databricks and McKesson use Tines AI-powered workflows to cut risk, reduce tech debt and focus where it counts.
exhibitors.roast: AI-powered workflows are if-this-then-that for people who wear fleece vests.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace one Tines playbook with a typed Python worker + queue; compare failure modes and monthly cost.

exhibitors.name: Token Security
exhibitors.description: Token Security is the leader in Identity-First AI Agent Security. Token Security delivers visibility, control, and governance of every AI Agent in your environment, so you can move faster and stay secure.
exhibitors.roast: Identity-first AI agent security is OAuth cosplay for chatbots that shouldn't have keys.

exhibitors.name: Tonic Security
exhibitors.description: Tonic accelerates prioritization and remediation of vulnerabilities and threats, with a Context-Driven Unified Exposure Management platform. Powered by Agentic AI and a security Data Fabric, Tonic contextualizes your attack surface, reducing risk to key business processes.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic unified exposure management is ASM with a thesaurus and a Series A slide.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run amass/subfinder + nuclei weekly, store diffs in SQLite, email delta; ship before Kubernetes operators.

exhibitors.name: Torq
exhibitors.description: Torq is transforming cybersecurity with its agentic AI security operations solutions. Torq empowers enterprises to instantly and precisely detect and respond to security events at scale.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic SOC is tier-1 macros pretending to be staff augmentation.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build three deterministic runbooks (isolate host, reset password, revoke token) with human approval gates.

exhibitors.name: Tracebit
exhibitors.description: Tracebit deploys and maintains tailored security canaries, proactively detecting intrusions across your organization - from cloud infrastructure, to identity and endpoints.
exhibitors.roast: Canaries across clouds: great until your bill is the real tripwire.

exhibitors.name: Trade Commission of Spain - ICEX
exhibitors.description: ICEX, is the Spanish body that promotes the internationalisation of Spanish companies. As part of the internationalisation process, the Spanish Pavilion at RSAC offers Spanish cybersecurity companies a great opportunity to take their first steps in this highly competitive market.
exhibitors.roast: A government booth: immune to disruption because disruption isn't in the procurement spreadsheet.

exhibitors.name: Transcend Information Inc.
exhibitors.description: Founded in 1989, Transcend Information Inc. is a global leader in digital storage, multimedia, and industrial products, designing, manufacturing, and marketing all in-house with a focus on quality and customer-driven innovation.
exhibitors.roast: Storage vendor at RSA: you're here because bits have mass and margins.

exhibitors.name: Tranxfer
exhibitors.description: Tranxfer is a modular suite for secure file exchange and storage, delivering full traceability, document lifecycle control, DLP and Shadow IT prevention, and regulatory compliance for highly regulated organizations.
exhibitors.roast: Secure file exchange is SFTP with compliance fonts and a watermark fetish.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use presigned S3 URLs + virus scan Lambda + audit log table; ship MVP before 'modular suite' dreams.

exhibitors.name: Trustero
exhibitors.description: Trustero's multi-agent AI for GRC orchestrates a team of intelligent agents to automate complex tasks with greater speed and accuracy, transforming how organizations manage their GRC programs.
exhibitors.roast: Multi-agent GRC is interns in a trench coat, but automated.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Generate control tests from YAML, run API checks weekly, store pass/fail artifacts—one agent max.

exhibitors.name: Trustmi
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Trustmi is the first Behavioral AI to empower security and finance teams to eliminate cyber-enabled fraud by seamlessly integrating across email, financial systems, and workflows. Before money moves, it detects attacks traditional tools often miss.
exhibitors.roast: Behavioral AI for finance fraud is rules that went to therapy and started journaling.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Start with DMARC strict + BEC keyword rules + dual approval on wires; measure $ blocked before ML.

exhibitors.name: Tufin
exhibitors.description: Tufin delivers risk visibility and helps enterprises simplify network complexity. As the leading Network Security Posture Management platform, Tufin serves as the unified control plane for modern hybrid networks spanning on-premises, cloud, SASE, microsegmentation, and multi-vendor environments.
exhibitors.roast: Network policy management is sticky because firewalls are organizational scar tissue.

exhibitors.name: Turbot
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Turbot prevents cloud misconfigurations through automated guardrails. Preventive controls block costly mistakes, eliminate alert fatigue, reduce risk, & stop teams from chasing incidents. Turbot sits alongside your CNAPP to prevent issues at scale.
exhibitors.roast: Guardrails for cloud misconfigs: Policy-as-Code for people who still misconfigure Policy-as-Code.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Adopt Open Policy Agent for three high-risk resources (S3 public, SG 0.0.0.0/0, IAM *:*); CI-eval on Terraform plans.

exhibitors.name: Tuskira
exhibitors.description: Preemptive, AI-driven security operations that simulate real attacks, validate defenses, and continuously close exploitable paths.
exhibitors.roast: Attack simulation for defense validation is purple teaming with a cron job and a PDF trophy.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Schedule Atomic Red Team in a lab VPC, capture detections, score coverage—skip cinematic 'AI-driven' trailers.

exhibitors.name: TuxCare
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor TuxCare secures the open-source software the world builds on – taking the risk out of innovation by keeping systems continuously protected, compliant, and running without disruption.
exhibitors.roast: Kernel live patching is unglamorous surgery on a running patient—technically gnarly, politically worse.

exhibitors.name: TÜV Informationstechnik GmbH
exhibitors.description: TÜV Informationstechnik (TÜVIT), based in Essen, Germany, is one of the world's leading service providers for Common Criteria (CC) evaluations of hardware and software and is authorized to perform evaluations in five different country schemes. We have successfully completed over 700 evaluation projects according to CC (from EAL1 to EAL7).
exhibitors.roast: Common Criteria lab: paperwork so rigorous it counts as a side-channel attack on your calendar.

exhibitors.name: TÜV SÜD
exhibitors.description: TÜV SÜD is a trusted partner of choice for safety, security & sustainability solutions. Over the last 150 years, we've added tangible value to our partners & customers through a comprehensive portfolio of testing, certification, auditing & advisory.
exhibitors.roast: Certification bodies print trust the old-fashioned way: stamps and liability umbrellas.

exhibitors.name: UPAS Corporation
exhibitors.description: UPAS has over 30 years of experience in delivering a one-stop internal network security platform that integrates NAC, IT asset management, mobile device management, and user identification to comprehensively protect enterprise cybersecurity.
exhibitors.roast: Three decades of NAC: vendor lock-in measured in cable drops and VLAN tears.

exhibitors.name: Ubiquiti Inc.
exhibitors.description: Ubiquiti delivers high-performance Wi-Fi, switching, routing, security, and IoT solutions through UniFi—an integrated platform that scales effortlessly with powerful software, thoughtfully engineered hardware, and enterprise-grade reliability, all without licensing fees.
exhibitors.roast: Networking hardware in a cloud security hall: you brought silicon to a YAML fight.

exhibitors.name: Unbound
exhibitors.description: Unbound lets teams unleash AI coding agents - without compromising on security. We detect every coding tool and MCP server running across your org, and apply governance controls to what agents can do and the code they produce.
exhibitors.roast: Detecting MCP servers is peak 2026: security for the IDE accessories aisle.

exhibitors.name: Unosecur GmbH
exhibitors.description: Unosecur provides a unified identity fabric for runtime visibility and control of all users, non-human identities, and their activities across multi-cloud, SaaS, identity providers, and on-prem.
exhibitors.roast: Unified identity fabric is CIEM with a knitting metaphor and the same API sprawl.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Inventory cloud identities via provider APIs, flag unused keys >90d, export CSV for audits—ship before 'fabric' poetry.

exhibitors.name: UpGuard
exhibitors.description: UpGuard's CRPM platform is powered by agentic AI, delivering unified intelligence across your attack surface, workforce, and vendor ecosystem. It turns billions of signals into actionable insights, helping teams resolve risk and strengthen resilience.
exhibitors.roast: CRPM with agentic AI is port scans plus a press release about graphs.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use Shodan/Censys + vendor questionnaires in Airtable; automate score only where data is objective.

exhibitors.name: Utimaco Inc
exhibitors.description: Utimaco is a global platform provider of trusted Cybersecurity and Compliance solutions and services. Develops on-premises and cloud-based HSMs, solutions for key management, data protection, identity management
exhibitors.roast: HSMs and PQC: when your threat model includes physics, you're not 'moving fast and breaking things'.

exhibitors.name: VIAVI Solutions
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor VIAVI Solutions delivers NetSecOps visibility through advanced network performance monitoring and packet-level forensics, enabling IT and security teams to accelerate detection, investigation, and resolution with shared, data-driven insight.
exhibitors.roast: Packet forensics at booth speed: the rare vendor whose demo can actually survive a skeptical wireshark nerd.

exhibitors.name: Valence Security
exhibitors.description: Valence protects organizations from SaaS and AI sprawl with unified discovery, SSPM, AI governance, AI agent security, ITDR, and flexible remediation options.
exhibitors.roast: SSPM plus AI sprawl discovery is OAuth inventory with anxiety pricing.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Use SaaS OAuth audit logs + Google Workspace admin reports; build a revoked-token drill before another dashboard.

exhibitors.name: Vanta
exhibitors.description: Vanta is the leading Agentic Trust Platform that helps over 15,000 businesses earn and prove trust. Companies including Atlassian, Duolingo, Golden State Warriors, Icelandair, Ramp and Synthesia rely on Vanta to earn and prove trust continuously.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic trust platform is SOC 2 automation with a chatbot that files tickets to your soul.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Automate evidence pulls from GitHub/Google/Okta; version-control policies; only then add LLM summaries for auditors.

exhibitors.name: Varonis
exhibitors.description: Varonis is the leader in data security. Our cloud-native Data Security Platform continuously discovers and classifies critical data, removes exposures, and detects threats.
exhibitors.roast: Your core product -- 'who has access to what file and should they' -- is a graph traversal query that gets harder at enterprise scale. The data classification engine is basically regex + ML that an LLM now does better out of the box. You're surviving on the sheer pain of crawling petabytes of unstructured SharePoint hellscapes that nobody else wants to touch. That's a moat, but it's a moat made of suffering.

exhibitors.name: Veeam Software
exhibitors.description: Veeam is the Data and AI Trust Company, ensuring data and AI are understood, secure, and resilient to enable safe AI at scale. As a leader in data resilience and security, Veeam protects 550,000+ customers worldwide, including 82% of the Fortune 500.
exhibitors.roast: Backup with a spreadsheet of logos: 550k customers still wake up to the same immutable restore demos.

exhibitors.name: Vega Security
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Vega unchains detection from storage. Tap every data source where it lives to hunt, investigate and stop threats fast. Go live in days, cut storage bills, run battle-ready detections, and prove impact without migrating or ripping out what works.
exhibitors.roast: Another 'SIEM without the baggage' pitch that still ends with you paying for storage and a team to tune it.

exhibitors.name: Vehere Technologies Pvt. Ltd.
exhibitors.description: Vehere, a global leader in Cyber Network Intelligence uses its deep COMINT/SIGINT expertise to secure defense/intelligence agencies, governments, critical infrastructure, and Fortune 500 companies against real-time cyberattacks with unmatched speed.
exhibitors.roast: COMINT/SIGINT cosplay for the trade floor—this is one of the few booths where 'we read the wire' isn't a metaphor.

exhibitors.name: Venice Security
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Control access for every identity in any environment with zero standing privilege. Valkyrie replaces static workflows with live, adaptive policies for human and non-human identities- via a unified, agentless, disruption-free platform.
exhibitors.roast: Adaptive PAM with a mythological name: your standing privilege problem becomes a standing licensing problem.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build a minimal PAM mock: JIT elevation via SSO groups, session recording to S3, break-glass workflow with Slack approval and 4-eyes, audit export JSON; ship a CLI + tiny web UI in a week.

exhibitors.name: Veracode
exhibitors.description: Veracode is a global leader in Application Risk Management for the AI era. Powered by trillions of lines of code scans, Veracode is trusted by organizations worldwide to maintain secure software from code creation to cloud deployment.
exhibitors.roast: Trillions of scans, infinite findings, and the same Jira tickets from 2014.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Wire Semgrep + OSV-Scanner + Trivy into GitHub PR comments; dedupe by rule+file; one 'fix recipe' link per finding; no enterprise portal.

exhibitors.name: Verax
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Verax Protect helps organizations ensure that the risks of shadow AI, such as the leakage of proprietary and sensitive data, malicious AI-generated responses, and data oversharing are prevented.
exhibitors.roast: Shadow AI panic as a SKU: finally, software that tells you people pasted secrets into ChatGPT—news at eleven.

exhibitors.name: Versa Networks
exhibitors.description: Versa Networks, the leader in SASE, combines extensive security, advanced networking, full-featured SD-WAN, genuine multitenancy, and sophisticated analytics via the cloud, on-premises.
exhibitors.roast: SASE bingo with real routers attached: you are buying plumbing, not vibes—still, the deck is 80% 'single pane'.

exhibitors.name: Veza from ServiceNow
exhibitors.description: Veza is the leader in identity security. We are the Enterprise Agent Identity Control Plane. Our Access Graph maps who and what can access what across your entire enterprise — humans, NHIs, and AI agents — across 325+ integrations. Trusted by Blackstone, Capital One, and Snowflake.
exhibitors.roast: 325 integrations to prove what IT already knows: everyone is admin somewhere.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build an access graph MVP: nightly IAM+SaaS API pulls into SQLite/Neo4j; query 'who can reach this bucket'; export CSV for auditors.

exhibitors.name: Vijil
exhibitors.description: Vijil tackles the central issue with enterprise AI today: can enterprises trust the agents they deploy? Vijil’s modular platform allows enterprises to define, evaluate, enforce, and improve the security, safety, and reliability of AI agents - cutting time to trust by 4x across the agentic lifecycle.
exhibitors.roast: Enterprise AI trust modular enough to invoice quarterly.

exhibitors.name: VirtualMetric BV
exhibitors.description: VirtualMetric delivers an intelligent security data pipeline that reduces SIEM costs by up to 90%, automates enrichment, normalization, and routing, and provides clean, high-fidelity data ready for analysts and AI-driven detection.
exhibitors.roast: 90% SIEM cost reduction is code for 'we move logs to cheaper buckets and hope nobody asks about detection'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship a log pipeline: Fluent Bit → Kafka/Redpanda → Iceberg/Parquet + DuckDB queries; basic alerts via scheduled SQL; bill as 'SIEM tax relief'.

exhibitors.name: Virtue Security
exhibitors.description: Virtue Security is a boutique pentesting firm specializing in complex technology assessments. Our process is tailored to technology focused enterprises who demand top-tier skillsets.
exhibitors.roast: Boutique pentest: the moat is hourly rates and a PDF with a cool font.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Productize a scoped assessment: Terraform lab, OWASP ASVS checklist runner, 10-page auto-report from JSON findings + executive summary template.

exhibitors.name: Vorlon
exhibitors.description: Vorlon is the Agentic Ecosystem Security Platform. We protect sensitive data in motion across the converged SaaS and AI environment where agents, APIs, integrations, and non-human identities operate at machine speed. Security teams use Vorlon to gain control, prevent breaches, and scale AI safely.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic ecosystem security sounds like ChatGPT wrote the category after three espressos.

exhibitors.name: WEEDSAND
exhibitors.description: As Korea's No. 1 solution provider for insider risk management and data protection, WEEDSAND has earned the trust of over 1,000 customers and positioned themselves as the leading force in the national security industry for 23 years.
exhibitors.roast: Korea's #1 insider risk story is still 'we watched USB and called it AI in 2010'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build insider-risk-lite: DLP on endpoints via OS logs, exfil patterns to Slack alerts, HR offboarding checklist automation; skip the national pride slide.

exhibitors.name: Wallarm
exhibitors.description: Wallarm is the API security platform that is the fastest, easiest, and most effective way to stop API attacks against APIs and AI powered applications.
exhibitors.roast: Fastest, easiest API security until the next fastest, easiest API security with a new logo.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up an API gateway (Envoy/NGINX) + WAF rules + OpenAPI diff in CI; block obvious authz bugs with contract tests; ship a dashboard of blocked paths.

exhibitors.name: Wasabi Technologies
exhibitors.description: Wasabi delivers cyber-resilient cloud object storage with immutability, air-gapped options, and rapid recovery—simple, fast, and affordable. S3-compatible with predictable pricing to harden backups and bounce back from ransomware.
exhibitors.roast: Immutability theater with a hard drive bill: cheap storage cosplaying as ransomware insurance.

exhibitors.name: Whistic
exhibitors.description: Whistic is the agentic vendor risk management platform built for modern security teams. AI agents automate vendor assessments, and the Trust Center Exchange eliminates questionnaires—accelerating TPRM, reducing friction, and scaling trust fast.
exhibitors.roast: Agentic vendor risk: questionnaires that now autofill wrong faster.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Automate vendor review: questionnaire YAML schema, evidence uploads to S3, scoring rubric in code, SOC2 mapping CSV export.

exhibitors.name: WiSECURE
exhibitors.description: Founded in 2019, WiSECURE focus exclusively on hardware security and build multiple HSM form factors for deployment across diverse environments — from government and enterprise systems, to edge and IoT devices.
exhibitors.roast: Actual keys in actual metal: refreshingly not a dashboard with a gradient.

exhibitors.name: WideField Security
exhibitors.description: WideField’s patented technology provides comprehensive visibility, rapid risk reduction, and real-time session detection and response across AI agents, NHIs, and human identities. We remove the fear of identity-driven threats, empowering teams to innovate with full visibility and zero hesitation.
exhibitors.roast: Patented visibility for NHIs and AI agents—translation: expensive correlation with a ribbon on it.

exhibitors.name: WitnessAI
exhibitors.description: WitnessAI delivers the only enterprise AI security platform with runtime security, complete network-level visibility of shadow AI tools, intent-based policy controls, and AI threat prevention.
exhibitors.roast: Runtime AI security is the new CASB: broad claims, narrow packets.

exhibitors.name: Wiz
exhibitors.description: The Wiz Platform secures everything organizations build and run in the cloud, enabling organizations worldwide to rapidly identify and remove critical risks from code, to cloud, to runtime. With Wiz, organizations can democratize security with a unified foundation across the complete development lifecycle that enables them to build apps faster and more securely.
exhibitors.roast: Code-to-cloud-to-runtime until your CFO asks why you also bought three other CNAPPs—then it's graph magic.

exhibitors.name: Wiz No AI Zone
exhibitors.description: Need a breather from all the AI talk? Step into the Wiz No-AI Zone, located steps away from the Moscone entrance. Take a moment to recharge, refuel with a fresh cup of coffee, and challenge a friend to a board game. No algorithms, just great vibes.
exhibitors.roast: Gold sponsor builds a 'no AI' room—peak 2026: even the coping strategy is branded.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Replace the activation with a local-only coffee cart and a laminated 'airplane mode' sign; same serenity, zero vendor swag budget.

exhibitors.name: Wolters Kluwer TeamMate
exhibitors.description: Wolters Kluwer TeamMate delivers a unified GRC management solution that brings governance, risk, and compliance together in a single, connected source of truth.
exhibitors.roast: Unified GRC single source of truth—enterprise speak for 'one spreadsheet to rule them all'.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Ship a minimal GRC tracker: risks/controls/issues in Postgres, approvals via email webhooks, PDF audit pack export.

exhibitors.name: X-PHY Inc
exhibitors.description: X-PHY Inc is a pioneer in intelligent, agentic, hardware-based cybersecurity solutions. The X-PHY SSD is AI-embedded and autonomously protects endpoint data without need for updates. Our X-PHY Deepfake Detector is on-device and detects in real-time.
exhibitors.roast: AI SSD with deepfake detection: firmware cosplaying as a SOC analyst—still, it's silicon, not slides.

exhibitors.name: XBOW
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor XBOW is the autonomous offensive security company redefining cyber defense for the AI era. Combining AI reasoning with offensive security workflows, the XBOW delivers expert-level security testing at machine speed.
exhibitors.roast: Autonomous offense at machine speed—great for demos, terrifying for your change-management board.

exhibitors.name: XM Cyber
exhibitors.description: XM Cyber is a leader in hybrid cloud exposure management. See how attackers leverage and combine misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, identity exposures, and more, across AWS, Azure, GCP and on-prem environments to compromise your critical assets.
exhibitors.roast: Attack path analysis for hybrid cloud: useful graph, shrinking differentiation as every CSPM adds a line chart.

exhibitors.name: Xeris AI
exhibitors.description: Xeris secures Agentic AI by providing enterprise visibility and control across AI Agents and MCP workflows. Powered by autonomous Super Agents, Xeris detects and shuts down Shadow AI, enforces policy in real time, and governs AI behavior directly at the Agentic layer.
exhibitors.roast: Super agents hunting shadow AI: recursive buzzword nesting detected.

exhibitors.name: Xint by Theori
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Theori is a cybersecurity firm specializing in offensive security research, red teaming, and AI-driven security solutions.
exhibitors.roast: AI-driven offensive security still ends with a human writing 'valid finding' in the report.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Glue ZAP/Burp scan output to a templated markdown report + retest checklist; sell speed, not 'autonomous reasoning'.

exhibitors.name: Yottasecure, Inc
exhibitors.description: Yottasecure cuts through CVE noise with real-time, contextual risk intelligence. Our AI identifies which CVEs are truly exploitable by correlating asset context, threat signals, and business impact—so security teams prioritize remediation and reduce risk faster.
exhibitors.roast: Contextual CVE intelligence—CVE triage as a service, starring your backlog.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Build an SCA triage bot: EPSS+reachability+deploy flag; PR comment with 'ship/snip' recommendation; ignore everything else.

exhibitors.name: Yubico
exhibitors.description: Yubico is the inventor of the YubiKey, the gold standard in phishing-resistant multi-factor authentication (MFA). The company is a pioneer in delivering hardware-based passwordless authentication using the highest assurance passkeys.
exhibitors.roast: Congratulations on being one of the few companies at RSA whose moat requires a semiconductor fab to cross. No amount of vibe coding is going to 3D-print a secure element. You're safe -- until someone figures out how to ship a YubiKey as a VS Code extension.

exhibitors.name: Zafran Security
exhibitors.description: Zafran is the first AI-native Threat Exposure Management platform that proves 90% of your critical vulns aren’t exploitable and then rapidly mitigates and remediates the 10% most likely to cause an incident using your existing security tools.
exhibitors.roast: AI-native threat exposure management: finally, TEM with vibes and fewer spreadsheets—still TEM.

exhibitors.name: Zenity
exhibitors.description: As the first unified security and governance platform purpose-built for AI agents everywhere, Zenity delivers full-lifecycle, defense-in-depth from discovery and posture management to detection and inline response. Learn more at www.zenity.io.
exhibitors.roast: First unified governance for AI agents—until next week's also-first unified governance.

exhibitors.name: Zentera Systems, Inc.
exhibitors.description: Zentera Systems secures modern enterprises with Zero Trust security, powered by Virtual Chamber technology. Deploy microsegmentation and remote access to protect IT, OT, and AI assets in hours, not months, with no network changes required.
exhibitors.roast: Microsegmentation in hours is a sales claim; OT reality is still 'we'll schedule maintenance in Q4'.

exhibitors.name: Zerac
exhibitors.description: Zerac is a zero-trust security platform that protects critical resources with end-to-end encryption on every interaction and low-latency direct connections between users, machines, and apps. It’s easy to deploy and adopt, and can be precisely tailored for organizations of any size or complexity.
exhibitors.roast: E2EE SDN zero trust: networking cosplay with a cryptography hoodie.

exhibitors.name: Ziggiz
exhibitors.description: Funded by DARPA, Ziggiz has created a 25x more performant PB scale security data lake unifying logs, context, and detections in seconds/minutes—not hours/days/months. Written on top of Databricks, Ziggiz supercharges threat hunting, IR, and compliance with a semantic layer and turnkey integrations
exhibitors.roast: DARPA-funded PB-scale lake on Databricks: impressive engineering, still a fancy SIEM warehouse with a grant header.

exhibitors.name: Zimperium
exhibitors.description: Zimperium is the world leader in AI-empowered mobile security. Purpose-built for mobile environments, Zimperium delivers unparalleled protection for mobile applications and devices, enabling organizations to stay ahead of today’s evolving threats.
exhibitors.roast: AI-empowered mobile security: finally, ML to tell you users sideload sketchy APKs—groundbreaking.

exhibitors.name: Zscaler
exhibitors.description: Zscaler is the global leader in zero trust security. Its cloud native Zero Trust Exchange™ platform protects thousands of customers from cyberattacks and data loss while accelerating digital transformation for the modern enterprise.
exhibitors.roast: Global cloud proxy at scale: the moat is miles of fiber and peering, not your slide deck.

exhibitors.name: Zynap
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Zynap is redefining cybersecurity with the first AI agent workflow platform for pre-emptive defense: automating workflows, unifying tools, and turning intelligence into proactive action. Powered by intel, driven by action.
exhibitors.roast: AI agent workflow for pre-emptive defense—SOAR with a ChatGPT internship.

exhibitors.name: alphaMountain.ai
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor Threat intelligence for domains and IPs, including malicious risk, web reputation and content classification. We use proprietary machine learning models for real-time detection APIs & feeds
exhibitors.roast: Domain/IP reputation with 'proprietary ML' is a race to stale lists, API rate limits, and who can market 'real-time' the loudest.

exhibitors.name: bitCorp
exhibitors.description: BitCorp builds transparent cybersecurity ensuring full data sovereignty. Now an international group with a Silicon Valley holding, advancing BIOS-level security, post-quantum cryptography and quantum-resistant hardware through global partnerships.
exhibitors.roast: BIOS and post-quantum in one sentence: either firmware sorcery or three slides of future tense.

exhibitors.name: cside
exhibitors.description: The web security company built natively on browser signals, not network packets like a firewall The platform for browser-side intelligence Client-side script monitoring, AI agents and VPN detection, Fingerprints, Fraud signals, chargeback evidence
exhibitors.roast: Client-side script drama: you are selling panic over third-party tags nobody will remove anyway.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: CSP report-only mode, SRI on critical scripts, nightly DOM diffs—call it ‘monitoring’ and move on.

exhibitors.name: d-Matrix
exhibitors.description: d-Matrix delivers memory-centric AI acceleration for real-time security inference. Its architecture enables low-latency, cost-efficient AI inspection, classification and enforcement at data center scale, built for modern LLM security workloads.
exhibitors.roast: Memory-centric AI silicon: real watts, real fabs’ moods, not a prompt.

exhibitors.name: digiDations
exhibitors.description: digiDations is a cybersecurity innovator developing AI-powered Adversarial Exposure Validation solution to anticipate and prevent breaches. Its solutions continuously validate defenses against real-world threats to enabling measurable confidence.
exhibitors.roast: AI adversarial exposure validation: BAS with a Singapore accent.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Run Atomic Red Team safely in lab, map to MITRE, track delta month-over-month.

exhibitors.name: eSentire
exhibitors.description: The Global Leader in MDR — Put prevention at the heart of your security program with eSentire’s Next Level MDR. Get enterprise grade technology, 24/7 expertise and decades of security leadership.
exhibitors.roast: MDR with 'prevention-first' is still humans in a SOC plus playbooks your intern could script.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up a toy SOC: ingest alerts via webhook, dedupe, enrich with GeoIP/VT, route to on-call rotation in PagerDuty/Opsgenie API, weekly metrics in Grafana.

exhibitors.name: enQase
exhibitors.description: First-Time Exhibitor enQase is the U.S.-based, full-stack quantum-safe security platform unifying proven cryptography, quantum hardware grounded in physics, and a powerful integration layer to deliver comprehensive protection and crypto agility for the quantum era.
exhibitors.roast: Quantum-safe plus 'hardware grounded in physics' is the rare booth where the demo might need a lab coat, not a README.

exhibitors.name: iDox.ai
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor iDox.ai delivers AI-powered redaction and data privacy tools—including iDox.ai Privacy Scout—to detect, classify, and remove sensitive information, protecting organizations from data exposure to LLMs.
exhibitors.roast: LLM redaction for privacy is the product eating the problem it created—convenient synergy.

exhibitors.name: iT.eam
exhibitors.description: iT.eam is a Dutch company with Brazilian roots, offering personalized solutions to protect businesses against digital threats. With flexibility, cutting-edge technology, and excellence, we ensure security and efficiency of our clients' assets.
exhibitors.roast: Personalized cybersecurity from a pavilion booth is managed SIEM with a passport—fine services, zero IP moat.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Productize one vertical (e.g., M365 logging) into fixed monitoring tiers with canned runbooks.

exhibitors.name: iZOOlogic
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor iZOOlogic delivers AI-driven external threat intelligence, attack surface management and third-party risk management. Trusted and accredited by Government and Financial Regulators.
exhibitors.roast: AI-driven threat intel and ASM for regulators is the same external scans with a stiffer PDF footer.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: External asset inventory + leaked cred monitoring + regulator-friendly monthly brief template.

exhibitors.name: iboss
exhibitors.description: iboss is a complete zero trust SASE Platform. Replace your entire legacy security stack with one unified platform. AI-powered CASB, advanced DLP, secure web gateway, ZTNA, browser isolation, and SD-WAN - all delivered from the cloud with unlimited capacity.
exhibitors.roast: Zero-trust SASE that replaces the whole stack is still SWG + CASB + ZTNA in a trench coat.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: Stand up Cloudflare/Zscaler-class policies for egress + identity-aware proxy; prove parity on three apps and migrate one pilot.

exhibitors.name: imper.ai
exhibitors.description: First Time Exhibitor imper.ai stops impersonation and social engineering attacks across every communication channel. We protect enterprises from help desk, executive and hiring threats utilizing deepfake and vishing by detecting the security signals in real time.
exhibitors.roast: Deepfake/vishing detection is one of the few places 'AI' isn't a red flag—adversaries move faster than compliance PDFs.

exhibitors.name: miniOrange
exhibitors.description: miniOrange delivers unified IAM (SSO & MFA), PAM, DLP, MDM, and CASB solutions. We secure 25K+ global organizations across Cloud, On-Premise, and Hybrid environments with competitive pricing and elite support.
exhibitors.roast: IAM+PAM+DLP+CASB combo platter: identity buffet until you’re bloated on SAML.
exhibitors.cursor_prompt: OIDC IdP stub (ory/hydra or zitadel) + RBAC model + sample SaaS SP integration; prove session hardening and MFA step-up.

exhibitors.name: pQCee
exhibitors.description: pQCee is a quantum cybersecurity startup. We design and build post-quantum products and solutions to strengthen and protect the $trillion digital economy against quantum attacks.
exhibitors.roast: Singapore pavilion PQC startup: booth geography beats algorithm novelty until NIST tests say otherwise.

exhibitors.name: six24 Cyber Labs
exhibitors.description: six24 Cyber Labs, LLC based in Maryland, delivers AI-driven cybersecurity solutions for adversary emulation and exposure validation. Our flagship platform, SIEGE, simulates real-world attacks to uncover exploitable risk and strengthen cyber defenses across government and enterprise environments.
exhibitors.roast: AI-driven adversary emulation—purple team GPT with a platform name that sounds like a workout.

exhibitors.name: wolfSSL
exhibitors.description: wolfSSL secures embedded and connected systems, protecting over 5 billion connections with lightweight (D)TLS 1.3 and cryptography. Our solutions provide secure communications, authentication, secure boot, and firmware protection for enterprise, automotive, aerospace, and military platforms. We offer FIPS 140-3 validated cryptography, WireGuard FIPS, FIPS kernel crypto, CNSA 2.0, and FIPS post-quantum cryptography support to meet current and emerging security requirements including the EU Cyber
exhibitors.roast: Five billion connections of tiny terror: TLS in places your linter fears.

exhibitors.name: zerothird
exhibitors.description: At zerothird, we revolutionize security with cutting-edge quantum cryptography (QKD). Our expert team develops science-backed, ultra-secure solutions for governments, finance, and critical infrastructure—making the world a safer place.
exhibitors.roast: QKD for governments: the booth where the threat model includes fiber and physics nerds.
